history.content:
<thought>
The cell directly in front (north) is lava, and the cell to the left (west) is a wall. The only safe adjacent cell is to the right (east), which is a floor. I will turn clockwise to face east so I can move forward.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north. The cell directly ahead is a floor cell (.) and is safe. There are no obstacles in that direction. Since the goal is not visible yet, I should move forward to explore more of the grid. The only adjacent safe cell is forward; left is lava and right is wall. So moving forward is the best option.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>The agent is at the bottom of the view, facing north. The cell directly ahead (north) is a safe floor cell ('.'), while the left cell is lava and the right cell is a wall. There is no goal visible in the current observation. To explore the map and find the goal, the safest immediate action is to move forward into the empty cell.</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me (north) is empty floor. The east is wall, west is lava, so the only safe move is forward. I will move forward to explore further.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
I can move forward into an empty floor cell (.) which is the only safe adjacent cell. This will allow me to explore more of the map and hopefully find the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom-center facing north. The cell directly in front (up) is a floor cell ('.'), which is safe. Moving forward will bring us to a new position and allow us to see more of the map. There is no visible goal yet, so exploring is necessary. I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
I am currently at the bottom row, fourth column, facing north. Directly in front of me is a floor cell, which is safe. To my left is lava, to my right is a wall. The only feasible move is to go forward to explore further. There is no goal visible yet, so I need to move into the unknown area.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
I am at the bottom row facing north, but the cell directly ahead is a wall. To my left (west) there are three floor cells ('.') that are unexplored. The goal is not visible, so I need to explore. Turning left (counter-clockwise) will allow me to move into those floor cells. Turning right would face a wall, which is not useful.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north and the goal O is directly north at the top of the column. The cell in front of the agent (row6, col4) is floor ('.'), so moving forward is safe. The entire column appears clear of obstacles. Therefore, the next action is to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me (north) is a floor cell (.), and the goal O is located further north along the same column. Moving forward will bring me closer to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing upward (^). The goal O is directly above the agent, four cells away. The cell directly in front (row6, col4) is floor (.), so it is safe to move forward. There are no obstacles in the immediate path.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom center of the view, with walls and lava blocking all directions except south, which is unknown and potentially traversable. Currently facing north, I need to turn twice to face south. I will start by turning clockwise to face east, then another turn to face south later. This avoids the immediate obstacles and opens up exploration to the south.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The goal O is to my left on the same row. The cell directly in front of me is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. I need to turn counter-clockwise to face left, then I can move towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
GIRA_ANTI_HORARIO
</action>

<thought>
The goal O is directly north of the agent, three cells away. The cell directly in front (north) is a floor cell ('.'), so it is safe to move forward. Continuing north will lead to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>
I am at the bottom row, facing up. The cell directly in front (above) is a floor ('.'), and the goal 'O' is two cells above that. Moving forward will bring me closer to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
FRENTE
</action>

<thought>The goal O is directly in front of me (north). I can move forward to reach it.</thought>
<action>FRENTE</action>
